title: What is DeHub?
html:
DeHub is an open source, user-owned and censorship-resistant social platform for creators and communities — a decentralized alternative to legacy apps like X, YouTube, Facebook and Rumble. Post, stream, message and go live like on any modern social app, except your content is minted on-chain the moment you upload it, your audience relationship belongs to you rather than an algorithm, and the platform's economics are shared with the people who actually create and consume the content.

Our mission is simple: give creators and their communities ownership of the platforms they build value on. Not a promise on a roadmap — a working product you can use right now at dehub.io on the web or with the DeHub app on Google Play. No wallet knowledge required to start; a wallet is created for you behind the scenes and gas is sponsored when you sign up with a social account, email or SMS.

Who's behind DeHub

DeHub isn't an anonymous token with a whitepaper and a promise. It's a shipping product built by a team of gamers, streamers and entertainment entrepreneurs, and operated by DeLabs Ltd, a UK-incorporated company with a real legal and compliance structure.

The team came together in 2021, assembled a core group of developers, and grew to more than a dozen people across three time zones within the first year. What matters most about how we started: DeHub shipped a fully built app before it ever launched a token. There was no venture round and no private presale funding development — the only fundraising was community liquidity generation on BNB, and from day one the community itself voted on the chain, the price, the supply and the exchange listings. That "product first, community-owned" DNA is still exactly how the project runs today.

Since then, the team has:

  • migrated 25,000+ token holders onto an upgraded V2 contract,
  • scaled live streaming to 50,000+ concurrent viewers on decentralized video infrastructure,
  • shipped end-to-end encrypted messaging, a custom sub-200ms CDN, on-chain tradable subscriptions and a Google Play app, and
  • been named Most Innovative Company at the UK's Corporate LiveWire awards, ahead of 900+ other companies.

A gaming and combat-sports pedigree

DeHub's gaming arm is staffed by developers from studios like Ubisoft, alongside a founding team deeply embedded in combat sports — people who have worked with UFC, Oktagon and PFL athletes, filmed documentaries with champions like Jorge Masvidal, and trained under Conor McGregor's long-time coach, John Kavanagh. That's the team building Last Chad Standing, billed as the first-ever MMA battle royale (lastchadstanding.com) — one of the first games of its kind on or off chain.

Why DeHub exists

Legacy social media has a structural problem: the platform owns everything. Your account can be deplatformed, your reach is decided by an opaque algorithm, your content can be demonetised without appeal, and the advertising value your attention generates is captured almost entirely by the house.

DeHub is built to invert that:

  • Chronological, transparent feeds — no shady algorithm deciding who sees your work.
  • On-chain content ownership — every upload is minted to your wallet. Nobody can silently delete your library or hold your audience hostage.
  • Creator-first economics — tokenized uploads, pay-per-view, token-gated content, on-chain tradable subscriptions, and ad revenue sharing that pays up to 90% out to the ecosystem instead of the house.
  • Open source — the code is public on GitHub, and the platform is designed so the community can fork, audit and build on it.
  • Privacy — end-to-end encrypted messaging, open sourced so the encryption claims can actually be verified.

How it works

DeHub feels like a familiar social app on the surface. Underneath, it runs on Web3 rails:

Content

Every video, stream or post is minted as an on-chain asset when you upload. Creators choose free, pay-per-view, or token-gated access per upload. Once created, content can earn ad revenue for life — passive, permissionless, censorship-resistant income that doesn't depend on you posting again tomorrow. Live streaming runs on decentralized video infrastructure that has scaled to 50,000+ concurrent viewers.

Subscriptions you actually own

Creator subscriptions on DeHub are on-chain and tradable. If a creator you backed early gets big, your early-supporter pass has real market value — you can sell it on. Creators earn a royalty on every resale. Contracts are live on Base, BNB Chain and Polygon.

The DHB token

$DHB is DeHub's ecosystem token, live on Base. It powers staking, subscriptions, tips, pay-per-view unlocks, governance voting and gaming rewards. You'll find it listed on CoinGecko and CoinMarketCap, and available directly on Coinbase. Governance uses a fair voting model where stake weight is capped to prevent whale manipulation, and each vote burns DHB to keep participation meaningful.

Gaming

DeHub's gaming arm has shipped a play-and-earn arcade sustained entirely by fee generation — no dilution or inflation needed to reward engagement — and is building Last Chad Standing, a fighter battle royale featuring licensed fighters from top fight leagues.

Where DeHub is headed

The 2026 roadmap builds on the same user-owned foundation: an AI toolkit for creator automation, a custom advertising stack with advanced targeting, phase 2 of the DePIN network, a fiat off-ramp, SDKs and a developer marketplace for third-party builders, and licensing deals bringing major fights and blockbuster content on-platform — with TV and games-console apps further out.

A quick history

  • 2021 — launched by a team of social media, gaming and entertainment entrepreneurs; rebranded to DeHub; the community voted on chain, price, supply and listing from day one, and the beta app passed 2,000 daily active users.
  • 2022 — UK entity DeLabs Ltd incorporated; marketplace, streaming and staking dapps shipped; V2 token migration completed for 25,000+ holders; recognised as Most Innovative Company at the UK's Corporate LiveWire awards.
  • 2023–2024 — creator economy tooling (90% ad revenue share, POVR ad tech), DePIN phase 1, on-chain tradable subscriptions, and the move to Base L2.
  • 2025–2026 — live streaming with animated tips, E2EE messaging, a custom CDN with sub-200ms latency, the Google Play launch, and the full web app at dehub.io.

Not the same as other "DeHubs"

The name collides with a few unrelated things — worth clearing up:

  • DePaul University's "DeHUB" (dehub.depaul.edu) is a student engagement portal for DePaul students. If you're a DePaul student looking for club sign-ups, that's your link.
  • Rowan University's "DEHub" is a digital engineering hub at an engineering school.
  • This DeHub — the one you're reading about — is the decentralized social platform at dehub.io.

Frequently asked questions

Who is behind DeHub?

DeHub is built by a team of gamers, streamers and entertainment entrepreneurs and operated by DeLabs Ltd, a UK-incorporated company. The project started in 2021, shipped a working app before launching any token, and has been community-governed from the start.

Is DeHub free to use?

Yes. Browsing, posting and streaming are free. A wallet is created for you behind the scenes — you can bring your own, but you don't need to know anything about crypto to start. Gas is sponsored for social, email and SMS signups.

What chain is DeHub on?

The DHB token and subscription contracts live on Base (Ethereum L2), with contracts also deployed on BNB Chain and Polygon.

How do creators earn on DeHub?

Tokenized uploads (pay-per-view and token-gated), tradable subscriptions with resale royalties, live-stream tips, watch-to-earn bounties, and a share of ad revenue — up to 90% of which flows to the ecosystem rather than the platform.

How is DeHub governed?

Through on-chain community voting. Stakeholder weight is multiplied within capped limits to prevent whale manipulation, and each vote burns DHB to discourage bad actors while rewarding meaningful participation.

Is DeHub really open source?

Yes — the code is public under the DeHubToken organization on GitHub, including the web app and the E2EE messaging implementation.

Where can I follow DeHub?

On DeHub itself at dehub.io, on X at @dehub_official, and on Telegram.
